title: Subjunctive in noun clauses
objectives: Use the subjunctive after verbs of wanting, doubting, asking
Recognize when 'que' triggers a subjunctive switch
grammar_notes: Pattern: [subject 1] + verb of wish/doubt/influence + 'que' + [subject 2] + SUBJUNCTIVE. Two different subjects required.
Same subject? Use the infinitive instead: 'Quiero comer' (I want to eat, same person), but 'Quiero que comas' (I want YOU to eat).
Russian 'чтобы' takes the past form ('чтобы он пришёл') — Spanish uses subjunctive. Same instinct, different morphology.
Affirmative 'creer que' takes INDICATIVE ('creo que viene' — I believe he's coming). Negative 'no creer que' takes SUBJUNCTIVE ('no creo que venga').
example_phrases: Quiero que vengas a la fiesta.
